acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Database initializes with expected tables and indices.
- [ ] WalIndexWriter and WalIndexReader trait methods are implemented.
- [ ] Schema version is stored and validated on open.
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: add WAL SQLite index with schema and reader and writer traits. There is no SQLite index today; WAL is a snapshot file. The realtime plan requires a rebuildable index for event lookup, idempotency, and watermarks.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.11 (schema decisions) and §6.2 (table layout)
- SQLite PRAGMA choices in §6.4
**Files:** src/daemon/wal/index.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/mod.rs, Cargo.toml (rusqlite)
design:
**Design**
- Implement schema for events, watermarks, client_requests, origin_seq, segments, meta (per plan).
- Add WalIndexWriter and WalIndexReader traits with methods from REALTIME_PLAN.md §6.1.
- Use UUIDs as 16 byte BLOBs. Store event_ids as CBOR encoded Vec<EventId> in client_requests.
- Configure SQLite with PRAGMA: journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL (configurable), foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout.
- Add schema version in meta table and enforce upgrade rules.
title: Phase 3: SQLite WAL index + WalIndex traits

closed_reason: PR review fix: peer quarantine recovery stays atomic and accepts same-seq realignment
description: Peer ACK divergence currently sets a sticky boolean that can permanently poison quorum accounting. Introduce explicit peer state machine with quarantine and recovery transitions; make permanent poison state impossible without explicit terminal reason.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Audit: /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs

JJ: myosorvp / baca7fe2 (bd-ovcb: make peer-ack divergence recoverable)

Changed: replaced sticky peer-ack diverged bool with typed quarantine state, preserved additive admin compatibility, recovered peers via validated HELLO/WELCOME snapshot or ACK advancement on the conflicted watermark, and kept quorum pending only while in-band recovery remains possible.

Verification:
- cargo fmt --all --check
- just dylint
- cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings
- cargo test
- cargo test -p beads-daemon --features test-harness
- cargo test -p beads-daemon-core
- cargo test -p beads-api
- cargo test -p beads-cli --lib commands::admin::tests -- --nocapture

Review: adversarial subagent review found real recovery/quorum and namespace-validation holes; all were fixed and final reruns were clean.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Audit note:
- Workspace: /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs
- jj change: xrvuwyul / c44aa2ef
- Scope: fixed follow-up PR review holes in peer quarantine recovery by keeping recovery atomic until all incoming watermark validation succeeds and by accepting same-seq realignment when the incoming head matches the previously expected head.
- Root cause 1: update_peer cleared quarantine before later durable/applied validation finished, so a subsequent Peer error could leave the peer healthy and partially mutate recovery watermarks.
- Root cause 2: same-seq realignment snapshots that restored the expected head could never recover quarantine because recovery required seq advancement only.
- Implementation: update_peer now validates durable/applied changes against cloned watermark maps and commits status plus maps only after the full update succeeds; recovery predicates now accept either seq advancement or same-seq expected-head realignment for both durable and applied quarantines.
- Added regressions for durable same-seq realignment, durable-side atomic recovery on later validation failure, applied same-seq realignment, and applied-side atomic recovery on later validation failure.
- Verification passed: cargo fmt --all --check; cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test -p beads-daemon-core -- --nocapture; cargo test -p beads-daemon --features test-harness; just dylint.
- Adversarial review: first rerun found two valid proof gaps on applied recovery coverage; both were fixed with additional regressions; final Einstein and Huygens reruns came back clean.

title: P0: Replace peer-ack diverged boolean with recoverable FSM

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Existing checkpoints import without repeated `WireLabelStateV1` decode warnings.
- [ ] Label state parity is preserved after import (no silent drop/regression).
- [ ] Version compatibility tests cover old and new encodings.
- [ ] Logs include actionable error context when an unknown future wire version is encountered.
description:
**Problem**
Daemon checkpoint import repeatedly logs decode failures for label state (`invalid length 0, expected struct WireLabelStateV1 with 2 elements`). This indicates wire-compat mismatch or invalid fallback handling in checkpoint decode.

Risk: noisy logs, extra recovery work, and possible state omission if decode fallback is incomplete.
design:
Make checkpoint decoding robust and explicitly versioned:
1. Audit wire schema/version negotiation for label state.
2. Add backwards-compatible decode path(s) for historical payload shapes.
3. Fail soft with typed diagnostics and targeted fallback instead of repeated generic warnings.
4. Add roundtrip and fixture tests across old/new checkpoint payloads.
title: Checkpoint label decode incompatibility spams warnings and risks state gaps
